9EEC51DDBB382CFD9AD6AA799CDA7F7E: Note: These options only enable the reporting paths. Either the Inception controller for SkyTunnel, or the T4000 must also be enrolled onto a monitoring station's Multipath IP system. Contact your monitoring station in order to set up this account.

A3DD97873A825F6EA45757AE6A76E531: Backup batteries should be fitted to the system to ensure the system can run without an AC supply for an extended period of time. AC supply to the system could be removed for a short period of time, for example, due to a brief power outage or brownout to the site or building maintenance that requires power to be removed for a short period of time. In these cases, power is restored quickly with little drain on the batteries, so the delay prevents the unnecessary notification to the monitoring station of the event. This in turn removes the need for the monitoring station to action the event and follow their response plan of informing the client for the short outage.
A4A2C37C0A6BF763C99C9A1C43654497: Enabling this option will allow for .csv files to be imported regardless of whether unmapped columns are found or not, and no error or system message will occur.
A6B4604488736015456D640018691DA6: To prevent too many emails from being sent and potentially filling an inbox or having the configured SMTP server marked as a spam server, Inception will attempt to group messages together if they occur close together. Messages will be grouped only if they are being sent to the same recipient address and have the same subject. Any messages that are grouped will include the date and time they occurred within the message.
A71DD570A528BFB45D1AF67DC1A6F02E:
Connections can be optionally configured to have framing characters to denote the start or end of a message being sent or received. These framing characters also support escape sequences to aid in specifying special characters like newline or null characters. For example, setting the suffix for received messages to '\
\
' would require the other system to end all messages with a carriage return + line feed character sequence to signify that the message ends before those characters.

The reporting format is selected by the monitoring station.
B80B9EE8D87D6F2613DE32B955CACA9B: is a feature designed to limit the number of repeated input activations or module health issues that are sent to a monitoring station. Often, this option is used in case a detector device becomes faulty and begins repeatedly activating, preventing repeated false signals from being sent through. It can also be used to stop an intruder from intentionally activating an input many times and masking their movements in the flood of input events from that one input.

1C2F6F45BBB4451E0629A65E47D7226E: As bug fixes and additional features are added to the modules connected to the Inception Controller new firmware versions will be released. These firmware files will be available on the Inner Range website. (www.innerrange.com). The module firmware file is then copied to the Inception Controller and once uploaded, the upgrade process can begin. One or more modules can be selected to be upgraded simultaneously, allowing the upgrade to be done in batches if on a live site. This can take some time, do not power down the Inception Controller or the module during this upgrade.

2598B751EE500E980A6D2015417A59EE: Time periods are based on weekly recurring times, where the time periods are generally valid at the same times each week. Exceptions to these rules can be added to allow temporary changes to be made to the schedule without significant programming changes.
27366DB78DE7C856DC0E2BCD014645D8: In most circumstances exceptions and calendar exceptions will offer similar functionality, the major differences are that calendars can be programmed once and applied to many time periods, which saves programming time. Calendar exceptions also provided calculated dates, which offers much greater flexibility when trying to achieve otherwise difficult exception rules.
29987175B34F8940C2A1E481D396D16F: Note that you are not restricted in using one or the other of these exception settings, it's perfectly fine to have the time period use both exceptions and calendar exceptions. Overall, both exception types allow temporary modifications to be made to the time period, potentially well in advance, without needing to alter the standard weekly recurring times.

2C03E7CA5E00DC2BEC7BF22F75720957: The above failure time is designed to filter out brief LAN comms issues, but if the module remains offline for longer than the configured time then this option determines how the system responds. 349A5B511EBE0FB4D52D4E6201A6C386: Once the battery testing is triggered, all modules with a power supply connected, battery connected and battery testing enabled will be added to a queue for testing. Modules are tested one at a time so that if there is an AC fail mid test, only part of the system is vulnerable, not all of it.

406B1FE2DDBF651AD14FDE4E0C881177: As bug fixes and additional features are added to the Inception Controller new firmware versions will be released. These firmware files will be available on the Inner Range website (www.innerrange.com). The firmware file is then copied to the Inception Controller and once uploaded, the upgrade process can begin. This can take some time, do not power down the Inception Controller during this upgrade.
4084EAE79CE19594664592839E9A3AD1: This page will dynamically display the state of scheduled tasks in the Inception system. This page displays the scheduled task's state, the last time the task was run (if known), and the next time a task is due to run.
4AFE84AD06FC0E67A20F065A917A8F3F: This page is used to create and edit recurring calendar dates which are used as exceptions to the normal operation of time periods.
4BF2E8103926F621AA4758277CA0396E: Time periods are broken up into two sections. First are the weekly recurring times, where multiple time blocks across multiple days can be configured. These time blocks apply every single week to the time period, causing it to become active whenever the current day and time matches a time block. This could allow Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm to be configured, and every week the time period would be active between those times. The second section contains the exceptions to the rule, which can override the weekly recurring times.
534B3AB00D5BE90714E9842FEEEAB9FE: page. When the scheduled time occurs, all modules configured for battery testing will be added to a queue. Modules are tested one at a time so that if there is an AC fail mid test, only part of the system is vulnerable, not all of it. Testing will only start if the module is online, AC power is present, the battery state is not low or missing and no short is detected.
60185D6DF18CAAA2C12659B1A0C3667F: page). This task will run on a set of days specified by a separate Calendar item, at a specific time of day. Multiple Calendar/Time of Day combinations can be specified to make the task run at additional times.

691E25DD99AB63FC4672E5283C23A9CE: A list of individual time and date based exceptions which override the list of weekly recurring times (higher priorities will override lower ones). A5EEFA5329D3E3C21F335627D946A919: A list of exceptions which apply on the days when their associated calendar is active (higher priorities will override lower ones). A time period that contains calendar exceptions will have its active or inactive state overridden by the calendar exception on the days for which it’s configured.
AC729C36D2AD7194F68B66F18BE44383: Specifies the Calendar items and times at which the scheduled task should run. The scheduled task will run at the time specified, on the days provided by the calendar.
AF4680467D179646079820916545AFB8: Calendars can be applied to time periods as calendar exceptions. Calendars are focused on dates rather than days of the week and provide alternative start and end times to the regular recurring times of time periods for the dates defined within the calendar. Calendar exceptions are used when the same time change for a day should be repeated on multiple days throughout the year. The calendar can be updated, which could affect multiple Time Periods in a consistent way.
AF83463BB617D795F075534BD5A6B0BF: schedules will remember their next scheduled run time even if the Inception device loses power, so that when power is restored, tasks that missed their scheduled run time will be immediately executed as soon as possible.

BD160D02A0E69E175BE2D8E5C949A4B3: This page is used to create and edit time periods.
C1ABC06AC4C4AEC42EE38CD01C4ADD55: If the start time is earlier than the end time (e.g 9am -> 5pm), then the time period will be valid during those times on the day specified. Alternatively, if the start and end time are the same (5pm -> 5pm) or the end time is earlier than the start (10pm -> 2am), then the valid block will start on the day specified and end on the next day. This allows an easy way to specify time blocks spanning midnight. This means, for exmaple, 10pm -> 2am on a Wednesday will be valid from 10pm to midnight on Wednesdays, and valid from midnight to 2am on Thursdays. Similarly, if the start and end times match, 5pm -> 5pm on Wednesday will be valid for 24 hours from 5pm Wednesday to 5pm Thursday.
CBABAF0312B5DD3B4DD591BA2BC983DB: The system can be configured to sync with an NTP server, either from the internet or one locally on site. 4629B671C7012126509C0F8D10C95E48: During commissioning, when all hardware modules are configured and online, a LAN secure process is performed to identify all present modules. This could be part of maintenance such as replacing a damaged module, or could be compromising the site security by replacing an existing module with a fake version.

This allows connection to the Inception web interface without connecting your device to the site's network.

DEC7CD9EB43C646034513F8E1E9197E2: The Inception web interface is capable of being displayed in different languages through the usage of Inception Language Pack (.irlanguage) files. These files can either be supplied by Inner Range or the installer, or manually created and edited with the Inception Translation Tool, a separate stand-alone application. To create a language pack, the first step is to download a copy of the Inception System's language pack template file to import into the Inception Translation Tool. This includes all of the translatable text in the Inception system for the current firmware version.

The default setting is 2k2 + 2k2 or 2k2 + 6k8 which supports the resistors supplied with the Inception controller and the Inner Range expansion modules. If you are using a different End of Line resistor configuration, select the one that you are using for the site.
EE8BB96774B93B7B6FBEF7D8B76DE90C: TCP Connections can be configured to act as a server and listen for incoming connections on a specified port on the Inception device, or to act as a client and establish a connection to a specified server given an IP address and port number.
EFC7068E33999EFEB4B183D6B3F2FACB: - Allows a passphrase to specified for the LAN encryption key.
F98F70AA5387572BFE83C68A49D3A70F: Inception requires an email server to be configured in order to send email notifications. Currently, an SMTP server is the only option for sending emails, however in future there will be other server options.
FC632EA2E39B19B4CC9FB8CCCDA5C630: The hexadecimal key that will be used as the LAN Encryption Key for communicating with Ethernet LAN modules. The key must be exactly 32 characters long and can only contain the characters 0-9, A-Z and a-z.
FF92D4D86497271924898B69ACBEF4A1: Note: Only enable this option if this module is connected to a 12V Power Supply and not a dedicated PoE connection.
014C6513BBE1673740E73223A074776E: A storage block represents a group of storage units. This could represent an entire site with 100s of units, a single building of units in a multi-building site, a single section of a building, and so on. There can be as many blocks as required in a system.
